LIVE · ON-CHAIN · WORLD CUP 2026

Called It

Call it live. Prove it on-chain.

A live, on-chain-verified FIFA World Cup 2026 prediction game on Solana — the thrill of a casual bet, made provable and trustless.

Solana devnetPhantom walletReal SOL stakesLive match feed

Solana World Cup 2026 Hackathon — Demo Day  ·  July 2026  ·  called-it.netlify.app

THE MOMENT WE LIVE FOR

Billions watch. Millions want in on the outcome.

Every match is a stream of moments people would bet on — but the tools to do it are either untrustworthy or too slow.

5B+
people expected to engage with FIFA World Cup 2026
48
48 teams · 104 matches · one global live audience
$1T+
wagered on sports every year — mostly through houses you must trust
02 / 16
THE PROBLEM

Three ways to bet on a live moment — each broken in its own way.

🏦

Bets you must trust a house

Traditional bookmakers are live, but centralized and opaque. You never see the true odds, the settlement, or the money. You just have to trust them.

⏱️

Post-hoc markets aren’t live

On-chain prediction markets are provable — but they resolve after the fact. By the time a market settles, the moment you cared about is long gone.

🎲

Casual apps have no stakes

“Guess the score” social apps are fun and instant, but there’s no real stake and no proof. Nothing is on the line, so nothing is really at stake.

03 / 16
WHO WE BUILD FOR

Two fans, one unmet need.

🔥

The casual fan

Watches every match with friends. Loves the rush of calling the next goal out loud. Would happily put a small stake on it — if it were quick, fun, and mobile.

“I called that corner — now let me actually cash it in.”
🛡️

The skeptic

Enjoys a bet but refuses to trust a black-box house. Wants to see the odds, the stake, and the settlement. If it’s not verifiable, they’re out.

“Don’t tell me I won. Show me on-chain.”
04 / 16
THE SOLUTION

Called It — call live, stamp on-chain, settle provably.

1

Call it live

During the match, call what happens next — next goal, corner or card — before it resolves. Instant, mobile, in the moment.

2

Stamped on-chain

Your call is a real devnet SOL stake, verified on Solana by the backend and stamped with the transaction hash and block time.

3

Settled & paid provably

A settlement worker resolves the market off the live feed. Called it → paid in SOL by a real transfer. No house, no dispute.

05 / 16
HOW IT WORKS

From tap to payout — six steps, one live loop.

1Connect Phantom
2See a live match
3Make a call
4Sign devnet stake
5On-chain stamp
6Settle & payout
→ → →
Fastify + Postgres on Railway · Solana web3.js · TxODDS TxLINE feed · Vite + React 19 PWA06 / 16
THE DIFFERENTIATOR

Validated against the live feed. Proven on-chain.

Called It is the only experience that is both live and trustless.

Live / instant — You call during the match, before the event resolves — not a post-hoc market.

On-chain provable — Real stake and settlement, verifiable on Solana. No house you have to trust.

Feed-validated — Provable markets settle off the TxODDS live feed and are cross-checked on-chain via the TxOracle validate_stat program.

“You call it — the chain proves it.”
07 / 16
WHAT IT DELIVERS

Not a mockup — a working on-chain money loop.

100%
on-chain settlement of provable markets
Real SOL
devnet stakes & payouts — actual transfers, not points
Instant
calls placed live, during the match
Verifiable
every stake carries a real tx hash + block time
Proven end-to-end on Solana devnet — deploy green, live fixtures (Spain vs Argentina), real stake tx, prediction stamped, worker settling every 10s.
08 / 16
COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE

Live and trustless is an empty quadrant — until now.

On-chain provable / trustless  →
Live / in-the-moment  ↑

Traditional bookmakers

Live, but centralized & opaque. Trust the house.

Called It

Live AND on-chain provable. No house — the chain settles.

Social “guess the score”

Fun, but no real stake and no proof.

Post-hoc prediction markets

On-chain, but not live — settles after the fact.

THE OPEN SPACE

Everyone owns one property.

Nobody owned both live and trustless — the top-right corner.

Called It sits there alone.

Only Called It is live and trustless — you call it, the chain proves it.
09 / 16
MARKET & SCALE

Start at the World Cup. Replicate to every live stat feed.

FIFA World Cup 2026

104 matches · billions of live viewers · the launch moment

Any football league

domestic leagues run year-round — the same markets, always on

Any live sport & stat

basketball, tennis, esports — anything with a live stat feed

WHY IT SCALES

Markets are feed-driven — add a sport by adding a feed, not rebuilding the app.

Settlement logic is generic over any provable stat (goals, corners, points…).

On-chain rails mean no per-market banking, licensing bottleneck, or house float.

Mobile-first PWA — no app-store gate, instant global reach.

10 / 16
TECH & TRUST

A real pipeline — signed, verified, settled, and open.

Solana devnet

Phantom signs real devnet SOL stakes; the backend verifies each tx on-chain before stamping the prediction.

TxODDS TxLINE feed

Live match events ingested over SSE into feed_events — the source of truth for what actually happened.

TxOracle validate_stat

An Anchor program cross-checks the settling stat on-chain (advisory) before a market pays out.

Open & verifiable

Every stake carries its tx hash + block time. Anyone can audit any outcome on the public explorer.

Backend: Fastify + Postgres (Railway, Node 22) · Frontend: Vite + React 19 PWA, Feature-Sliced11 / 16

12 / 16
VIABILITY

Legally aware, technically proven, operationally lean.

⚖️

Legal

Framed as a skill-based, free-to-play game on devnet — no real-money gambling.

On-chain settlement is transparent and auditable by design.

Jurisdiction-aware rollout planned before any mainnet real-money launch.

🧩

Technical

Full loop already runs end-to-end: stake → verify → stamp → settle → pay.

Feed-driven, generic settlement — new markets/sports without a rewrite.

Standard, well-supported stack: Solana, Fastify, Postgres, React.

⚙️

Operational

Serverless-friendly deploy on Railway; worker loop is cheap to run.

No house float or per-market banking — on-chain rails carry the money.

Small surface, mobile-first PWA — fast to iterate and ship.

13 / 16
